text: Advice Discrimination at work Bullying at work If you've been discriminated against at work Handling bullying and discrimination Witnessing discrimination Preventing discrimination Questions about discrimination at work Hate crime at work Employers must not discriminate. Workers are protected by law (Equality Act 2010). If you witness or experience discrimination or bullying, you can choose what to do. Employers should take steps to prevent it, and take complaints seriously. What the Equality Act says. Direct and indirect discrimination. Harassment. Victimisation. What bullying is. What to do if you're being bullied. Employer responsibilities. What you can do. Informal and formal steps. How employers should deal with bullying and discrimination complaints. What to do if you think someone else at work is being discriminated against. How employers can stop discrimination happening at work. Public sector equality duty. What workers can ask their employer. How employers should answer. What hate crime is. Reporting it to the police. Handling complaints. If you like, you can tell us more about what was useful on this page. We cannot reply – so do not include any personal details, for example your email address or phone number. If you have any questions about your individual circumstances, you can contact the Acas helpline .
